confidenceBoundary.canProve: No supported accessible-name source (label, aria-label, aria-labelledby) was found in the analyzed source file for a given input element.
confidenceBoundary.cannotProve: The absence of a static label means the control is inaccessible at runtime. Framework-generated labels, dynamically injected ARIA attributes, or programmatic focus-name mappings may create accessible names outside the source text.

exclusions: Custom form controls with internal ARIA management

violation.gap: JSX, Vue, Svelte template variants needed

requiredTests: Suppression by rule and file pattern
acceptanceCriteria.cliBehavior: Lint triggers warning or error finding with evidence showing the unmatched input
acceptanceCriteria.performanceConstraints: Single-file scan, no network, no rendering
acceptanceCriteria.failureBoundaries: Does not scan generated output, node_modules, or binary files. Cap at MAX_SOURCE_FILE_BYTES.
acceptanceCriteria.documentation: Finding message must state: 'This check covers static accessible-name sources only. Framework-generated labels are not detected.'

confidenceBoundary.canProve: CSS @keyframes, animation, or transition declarations exist in the scanned source without a corresponding prefers-reduced-motion media query wrapper.
confidenceBoundary.cannotProve: The absence of a prefers-reduced-motion wrapper means motion is not handled at runtime. JavaScript animation libraries (GSAP, Framer Motion, anime.js), canvas/WebGL rendering, video autoplay, and runtime-injected styles are invisible to static CSS analysis.

acceptanceCriteria.cliBehavior: Lint triggers warning finding with evidence showing the unmatched animation declaration
acceptanceCriteria.performanceConstraints: Single-file scan, no network, no rendering
acceptanceCriteria.failureBoundaries: Does not analyze JavaScript runtime animation libraries. Does not detect video autoplay.
acceptanceCriteria.documentation: Finding message must state: 'This check covers CSS-detectable animation only. JavaScript animation libraries (GSAP, Framer Motion) are not analyzed.'

confidenceBoundary.canProve: A statically resolvable CSS color/background-color pair in the same rule falls below the WCAG AA threshold when computed against the standard WCAG relative-luminance formula.
confidenceBoundary.cannotProve: The rendered text actually has that contrast ratio. Cascade, inheritance, opacity, images, gradients, pseudo-elements, and runtime values may produce different rendered contrast. A static finding is not a WCAG conformance failure.

requiredInputs: WCAG relative luminance computation (same-rule pairs only)

violation.gap: Additional opacity-cascade and custom-property tests needed

borderline.gap: Gradient case is explicitly out of static scope — good documentation fixture

requiredTests: Same-rule solid-color pair below threshold triggers finding
Same-rule solid-color pair above threshold produces no finding
acceptanceCriteria.cliBehavior: Lint triggers warning with evidence showing the color pair and computed ratio. Message must state scope limitation.
acceptanceCriteria.performanceConstraints: Single-file scan; contrast computation is O(n) over CSS declarations
acceptanceCriteria.failureBoundaries: Does not resolve CSS cascade, custom properties, or inheritance chains beyond direct parent.
acceptanceCriteria.documentation: Every finding message must include: 'This check covers only statically resolvable solid-color foreground/background pairs in the same CSS rule.'

confidenceBoundary.canProve: A recognized skip-link structure (anchor with href=# targeting a main-content element) exists and is positioned as the first focusable element in the source.
confidenceBoundary.cannotProve: Absence of a skip link means the page fails WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks). Proper heading structure, ARIA landmarks, or programmatic focus management may satisfy the requirement. A static scan cannot evaluate these alternatives.

exclusions: Single-content pages with no repeated navigation blocks

violation.gap: Must produce advisory finding, not error

borderline.gap: Should not trigger — demonstrates alternative bypass

requiredTests: Skip-link absence produces advisory, not error
SPA with focus management produces no finding
requiredFixtures: ARIA landmark page without skip link
acceptanceCriteria.cliBehavior: Absence finding must be warning-level with message about alternative bypass mechanisms
acceptanceCriteria.failureBoundaries: Does not evaluate ARIA landmark correctness. Does not verify skip-link target exists.
acceptanceCriteria.documentation: Finding message must state: 'A skip link was not detected. WCAG 2.4.1 may also be satisfied by ARIA landmarks or programmatic focus management.'

confidenceBoundary.canProve: At a defined mobile viewport width, an interactive element has a rendered bounding box smaller than 44x44 CSS pixels.
confidenceBoundary.cannotProve: Anything about rendered touch target size from static CSS analysis alone. CSS width/height/padding declarations may differ from rendered size due to cascade, content overflow, border-box, transforms, and responsive rules.

violation.gap: Requires browser rendering to determine actual touch area. Static CSS declarations are insufficient.

control.gap: Same limitation — rendered size may differ from declared CSS.

borderline.gap: Table density tradeoff requires rendered measurement.

requiredTests: Rendered target below 44x44 at mobile viewport triggers finding
Rendered target above 44x44 produces no finding
Responsive size change at different breakpoints
acceptanceCriteria.cliBehavior: Not applicable under current architecture. Requires browser-assisted or visual-review pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria.performanceConstraints: Browser rendering pipeline, not static file scan.
acceptanceCriteria.failureBoundaries: Desktop viewports excluded. Non-interactive elements excluded.
acceptanceCriteria.documentation: Document this rule as browser-required until rendered analysis infrastructure exists.

tranche1Rationale: Input labels have the cleanest detector path, definitive findings, WCAG Level A, and highest user impact. Reduced motion adds complementary accessibility coverage with a well-bounded CSS pattern and low false-positive risk.

deferredArchitecture.accessibility.skip-navigation-link: bounded-static + advisory — needs careful finding message

notes: This decision record assigns architectural disposition only. It does not modify rules/core.json, profile files, linter implementation, or detector code.
Severity recommendations are advisory. Final severity assignment occurs during implementation PR.
Profile recommendations are advisory. Final profile membership occurs during profile PR.
The 39 non-review-ready proposals require a separate disposition report.
Copy proposals require a policy decision about a separate copy-quality profile.

authorizedAnalysis: HTML-like markup: tag matching, attribute extraction, element-type classification, id/for reference matching
CSS source text: declaration block extraction, @media query scope detection, property/value pair matching
Component template syntax: attribute extraction (JSX, Vue, Svelte)
notSupportedByPatternMatching: label for/id association requires reference resolution beyond keyword matching
aria-labelledby resolution requires DOM-level reference chain traversal
media-query scope analysis requires block-level structural understanding
CSS cascade resolution requires full parser and specificity engine

acceptanceGates: Framework pattern tests pass alongside core tests
Documentation updated for new template syntax
